Solving 3d Gravity with Virasoro TQFT

Published

Collier, S., Eberhardt, L., Zhang, M.

Year
2023
Journal
SciPost Phys.
Volume / Pages
15, 151
arXiv 2304.13650 hep-th
DOI 10.21468/SciPostPhys.15.4.151

Abstract

We propose a precise reformulation of 3d quantum gravity with negative cosmological constant in terms of a topological quantum field theory based on the quantization of the Teichmüller space of Riemann surfaces that we refer to as Virasoro TQFT. This TQFT is similar, but importantly not equivalent, to SL(2,R) Chern-Simons theory. This sharpens the folklore that 3d gravity is related to SL(2,R) Chern-Simons theory into a precise correspondence, and resolves some well-known issues with this lore at the quantum level. Our proposal is computationally very useful and provides a powerful tool for the further study of 3d gravity. In particular, we explain how together with standard TQFT surgery techniques this leads to a fully algorithmic procedure for the computation of the gravity partition function on a fixed topology exactly in the central charge.
